title: Magical Peruvian Dulce De Leche Suspiro Limeno
description: A buttery, crispy, nostalgic Peruvian dish featuring yellow food coloring, beurre-monte, and Mediterranean-friendly ingredients, formed into a suspiro limeno and cooked in a tandoor clay pot.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the evaporated milk, granulated sugar, and unsalted butter, and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar has dissolved.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the mixture has thickened slightly.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the yellow food coloring and beurre-monte.
In a small bowl, whisk together the large egg yolks and vanilla extract.
Temper the egg yolks by slowly pouring the warm milk mixture into the egg yolks, whisking constantly.
Pour the mixture into the preheated tandoor clay pot and smooth the top.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the edges are set and the center is still slightly jiggly.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool to room temperature.
Cover the pot with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until chilled.
